summary: Gruber critiques iOS's Shake to Undo gesture as one of the platform's worst UI decisions. While system-wide Undo existing on iOS is valuable (Android lacks it entirely), the shake gesture is undiscoverable, bad for accessibility, and risks dropping your phone. He contrasts it with the Mac's universal Edit → Undo menu command available since 1984, and notes that iPad's keyboard Undo button only works for text editing. He suggests Apple should add an Undo button to the iPhone keyboard's suggestion row and shares an anecdote that Shake to Undo originated as a joke by an engineer that Scott Forstall took seriously.
key_points: Shake to Undo is impossible to discover through on-screen UI exploration, bad for accessibility, and most iOS users likely don't know it exists
The Mac solved Undo in 1984 with a universal menu command and keyboard shortcut — iOS still lacks an equivalent consistent mechanism
iPad's keyboard Undo button is better but only works for text editing, falling short of system-wide undo
The absence of a menu bar and keyboard shortcuts on iPhone makes implementing universal UI patterns like Undo genuinely difficult
Shake to Undo reportedly originated as an engineer's joke that Scott Forstall took literally and shipped
money_quotes: The fact that system-wide Undo exists in iOS is great. (Android doesn't have it.) But that it's a shake gesture is dreadful — impossible to discover through exploration of the on-screen UI, bad for accessibility, and risks your phone flying out of your hand.
How many iOS users even know about Shake to Undo? Very few, I suspect.
That's why the menu bar and keyboard shortcuts are such essential elements of the Mac experience.
Shake to Undo is problematic enough that I think Apple should have figured out something better for the iPhone by now.
There are lots of little things like this about iOS. It's a tricky problem how to implement universal Undo without a menu bar and without keyboard shortcuts.
One engineer joked that they could just make you shake the iPhone to invoke it. Forstall said he loved the idea, and what was proposed as a joke has been with us as the Undo interface ever since.
key_insight: Shake to Undo illustrates how iOS still lacks satisfying solutions to fundamental UI problems that the Mac solved decades ago with its menu bar and keyboard shortcuts.
